items.quote: First time trying tarot — the cards nailed my burnout. The +24% number matched the offer I just got. Already scheduled the interview 😂
items.name: T**

items.quote: Engineering 5–8 yrs Hanoi → +28%. Wish I'd done this 2 years ago. Stayed too long out of fear.
items.name: J**

items.quote: It's fun but oddly accurate. The cards picked 'different environment' which is exactly what I'd been thinking. Worth 29,000 VND.
items.name: M**

items.quote: Girlfriend told me to try. Tarot is tarot, but the number gives me confidence to go talk to my boss.
items.name: D**

items.q: How is this different from regular tarot?
items.a: Traditional tarot plus real data. The cards point a direction (timing · potential · path); the data gives a concrete salary upside number.

items.q: Where does the salary data come from?
items.a: Real Vietnam job-switchers from the last 24 months, broken down by role, experience, and city. If the sample is too small, no result is shown.
Tarot is tarot — for fun and reference. The salary data is real. But big decisions are still yours — not the 3 cards. Don't quit your job just for +28%.
Generally no — it's a digital result you've already seen. But if a payment error or technical glitch meant you couldn't view your result, email us and we'll sort it out.

disclaimer.title: ⚠ FOR ENTERTAINMENT
disclaimer.body: This is tarot. Treat it as a fun reference, not a career counselor. We're not liable if you switch jobs based on 3 cards and miss the old team's free coffee. ☕✦

topic.label: ✦ CAREER TAROT ✦
topic.question: What's on your mind right now?

options.timing:
Is now the right time
to switch jobs?
options.potential:
Will things actually
get better if I switch?

deck.label: ✦ Choose Your Cards ✦
deck.instruction: Hold your question in mind — then pick the cards you feel drawn to

result.label: ✦ The Cards Speak ✦

synthesis.timing: Read together, the three cards reveal a distinct window forming around you right now. The current beneath your career isn't random — it's been building momentum for a while, and this spread suggests the next 2–4 months hold unusually high leverage. How you use that window — whether you step through it or let it close — is the one variable the cards leave to you.
synthesis.potential: Taken as a whole, these three cards outline a capacity you haven't fully tested yet. There's a gap between what you're doing and what you're capable of — and the spread suggests that gap is wider than you think. The data in the next step will put a number on that gap. But the cards already say: you're underpriced.
synthesis.direction: Together, the cards sketch the shape of an environment where you'd actually thrive — not just survive. Your current setting may be comfortable, but the spread points toward a place with more room, more alignment, and more reward. The next question isn't whether to move — it's where that shape exists in the real market.

result.softCtaNote: Free — share role, experience, city, salary

interpretations.situation: Something familiar is already shaking. The role, the team, the routine you've built around yourself — it looks stable from the outside, but underneath, the cracks have been spreading for months. You've probably felt it: a quiet dread on Sunday nights, a sense that your growth flatlined a while ago. That feeling isn't burnout — it's the foundation telling you it can't hold what you're becoming.
interpretations.energy: An external force is pressing against the structure of your current position. This isn't gentle evolution — it's disruption that demands a response. The Tower doesn't ask if you're ready; it clears what's blocking you whether you volunteer or not. In career terms, this often means a restructure, a management change, or an industry shift that makes staying still more dangerous than moving.
interpretations.outcome: After the collapse comes clarity you couldn't have reached from inside the old structure. People who draw The Tower in outcome position often look back 6–12 months later and say, 'That was the best thing that happened to me.' A sturdier path is already forming — one that matches who you are now, not who you were when you took the old role.

interpretations.situation: You're standing at a cyclical turning point — the kind that comes once every few years. The industry you're in, the city you're in, even the team you're on — the conditions that held steady are now visibly shifting. What worked last year is losing traction. The current is changing direction, and your position on the wheel matters more than your effort right now.
interpretations.energy: The Wheel says timing beats hustle. There are moments when the market, your experience level, and hiring demand align — and this appears to be one of them. Forcing an outcome against the current wastes energy. Reading the current and positioning yourself where the wheel is headed is the move that pays off disproportionately.
interpretations.outcome: A new cycle is opening. Things that felt stuck — a stalled promotion, a salary that hasn't moved, a skill that had no buyer — start unlocking faster than you expected. The Wheel in outcome position is one of the strongest signals in the deck: the next rotation favors you, as long as you're not still clinging to the last one.

interpretations.situation: You've been through a stretch that wore you down — maybe a toxic manager, a project that went nowhere, or a long period of feeling invisible. The Star appears at the tail end of that darkness. The worst is behind you, even if you can't fully see the other side yet. A small light is already filtering in — a conversation, a job post, a restless thought that won't go away.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of recovery, renewal, and quiet confidence rebuilding. The Star doesn't shout — it glows steadily. It says: the doubt you've been carrying ('Am I good enough? Is it too late?') is not grounded in reality. Your skills, your experience, your instincts — they haven't eroded. They've been waiting for a context worthy of them.
interpretations.outcome: Possibilities that seemed unreachable — a role at that company, a salary in that range, a team that actually values your work — turn out to be closer than you thought. The Star in outcome position means a peaceful, almost surprising arrival. Not a battle won, but a door that was open all along.

interpretations.situation: You've reached the ceiling of your current chapter. The learning curve has flattened, the challenges don't challenge you anymore, and there's almost nothing left to absorb from this environment. This isn't failure — it's graduation. The restlessness you feel isn't a problem to fix; it's a signal that you've outgrown the container.
interpretations.energy: The World card carries the energy of integration — pulling together everything you've learned, every skill you've stacked, every hard lesson absorbed. But it also demands closure before expansion. If there are loose ends — an unfinished project, an unspoken conversation, a commitment you made — tying those off is what unlocks the next stage. Don't skip that step.
interpretations.outcome: One cycle completes and a larger one begins — simultaneously. The World in outcome position is the rarest and most complete signal in the deck. It doesn't just say things will get better; it says you'll step into a version of your career that makes the previous chapter look like a prologue. The scale changes. The stakes rise. And you're ready for it.

interpretations.situation: Deep down, there's an urge that won't be reasoned away — a pull to start fresh. Maybe it's a different industry, a different city, or a role you've never tried. You can't fully articulate it yet, and that's what makes it scary. But The Fool appears precisely when the unnamed pull is more trustworthy than the named plan.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of lightness, curiosity, and the beginner's mind. The Fool says: your need to have it all figured out before you move is the thing slowing you down. The first step doesn't require a 5-year plan — it requires an honest 'yes' to the question you've been avoiding. Action creates clarity. Not the other way around.
interpretations.outcome: A choice that looks reckless from the outside works out far better than the safe option would have. People who draw The Fool in outcome position tend to surprise themselves — and the people who doubted them. The new road doesn't just appear; it rises to meet you, as if it had been waiting for you to finally take the step.

hermit.name: The Hermit
hermit.meaning: The answer is already within you

interpretations.situation: You've been collecting opinions — from friends, LinkedIn posts, mentors, maybe even strangers on the internet. But the answer you're looking for isn't out there. The Hermit appears when you already know what you want to do; you're just looking for external permission to do it. You don't need permission. You need 10 minutes of honest silence.
interpretations.energy: This is a period of strategic withdrawal — not retreat, but deliberate self-inquiry. The Hermit's lamp burns inward. Before you update your CV, before you open a job board, the card asks you to sit with one question: 'If money and other people's expectations didn't exist, what would I do next?' The answer to that question is your compass.
interpretations.outcome: A deep, unshakeable clarity arrives — the kind that doesn't waver when someone questions it. The Hermit in outcome position means your decision will feel quiet and certain, not dramatic. And because it comes from your own center rather than external pressure, it holds up over time in a way that reactive choices never do.

interpretations.situation: The fog is lifting. A period of confusion, second-guessing, or being undervalued is coming to an end. Your view is clearer than it's been in a long time — you can see your strengths, your gaps, and your options with unusual honesty. The Sun appears when the conditions for visibility have finally aligned.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of vitality, confidence, and being seen. The Sun says: stop dimming yourself. Stop underselling your experience in interviews, stop accepting titles below your level, stop hedging when someone asks what you're worth. The more plainly you present yourself, the more the right opportunities find you. This is not arrogance — it's accuracy.
interpretations.outcome: A clear, undeniable success. Not a lucky break — a result that's directly proportional to the work you've put in. The Sun in outcome position is the deck's strongest positive signal. It says: the effort you've invested is about to show returns that others can see too. Offers, recognition, and options — in the open, not behind closed doors.

interpretations.situation: Something feels off, but you can't pin down what. Beneath the surface, anxiety and intuition are rippling at the same time, and it's hard to tell which is which. Maybe you're uneasy about your current role but can't say why. Maybe an offer looks great on paper but your gut hesitates. The Moon says: that unease is data. Don't ignore it.
interpretations.energy: The Moon's energy is the hardest to navigate — it's the energy of illusion, projection, and hidden information. Not everything you've been told is the full picture. A 'great culture' might mask dysfunction. A 'competitive salary' might be below market. Your task right now is not to decide — it's to gather the information that's been kept in shadow. Ask the questions no one wants asked.
interpretations.outcome: As the fog clears — and it will — the hidden truth reveals itself. The Moon in outcome position means a surprise disclosure: a fact about a company, a colleague's real intentions, or your own buried desire that finally surfaces. The truth may be uncomfortable, but it sets you free to act on reality instead of assumption.

interpretations.situation: Structure and authority are already defining your environment. You've built (or been placed in) a stable system — a predictable role, clear responsibilities, a reliable paycheck. But stability has a shadow: it can become a cage you stop noticing. The Emperor asks whether your structure is serving your growth, or just your comfort.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of strategy, systems, and long-term thinking. The Emperor doesn't chase trends — he builds positions. If you're considering a move, the card says: don't jump at the first shiny offer. Map the terrain first. What's the growth path? What's the 2-year trajectory? The Emperor's moves are slow, but they compound. This is how lasting career gains are built.
interpretations.outcome: Steadiness is rewarded — not with a flashy win, but with a seat that nobody can take from you. The Emperor in outcome position points to a promotion, a leadership role, or a position of genuine authority. It's a firm climb rather than a fast jump, and the ground beneath you stays solid.

interpretations.situation: Momentum has been building — maybe quietly, maybe chaotically — but the energy is ready to move. Multiple forces are pulling in different directions: stay or go, this industry or that one, safety or ambition. The Chariot appears when the time for deliberation is ending and the time for decision has arrived.
interpretations.energy: The Chariot's energy is pure focused will. It says: pick a direction and commit fully. Half-measures — sending a few applications while staying comfortable, 'exploring options' without deadlines — won't work in this phase. The card demands singular focus. One target. One timeline. One version of 'yes.' The scattered approach dissipates the very momentum that's your greatest asset right now.
interpretations.outcome: A decisive, frontal win. What you've been hesitating on — the conversation, the application, the negotiation — breaks open faster than you expected. The Chariot in outcome position is the card of people who look back and say, 'I should have done this sooner.' The door wasn't locked. You just hadn't pushed.

interpretations.situation: You're in a testing period. The pressure is real — whether it's a difficult boss, an uncertain market, or your own self-doubt whispering that you're not ready. You tremble, but the strength to endure is already there. The card doesn't appear for people who are weak. It appears for people who don't yet realize how strong they are.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of gentle, patient power — not force. Strength doesn't mean bulldozing through obstacles; it means taming the fear without letting it drive your decisions. In career terms: don't accept a lowball offer out of anxiety. Don't stay in a bad role because change feels scary. Your capacity to hold steady under pressure is your most valuable negotiation asset right now.
interpretations.outcome: A true overcoming — one that surprises you most of all. The Strength card in outcome position means you'll push past a limit you thought was fixed. A salary you thought was out of reach. A role you thought required more experience. A conversation you thought you couldn't have. The limit was never external. It was the story you told yourself about what's possible.

interpretations.situation: There's something you already know but haven't said out loud — maybe not even to yourself. Beneath the spreadsheets and pros-and-cons lists, a deeper knowing is stirring. The High Priestess appears when rational analysis has taken you as far as it can. The remaining distance can only be covered by listening to what your body already decided.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of intuition, hidden knowledge, and things unseen. The Priestess says: the information you need isn't on job boards or salary surveys. It's in the patterns you've noticed but dismissed — the coworker who left and thrived, the industry that keeps catching your eye, the role description that made your pulse quicken. Your subconscious has been doing research your conscious mind hasn't caught up to.
interpretations.outcome: Hidden information surfaces — an opportunity you didn't know existed, a connection that opens unexpectedly, or a truth about your current situation that changes everything. The Priestess in outcome position means the intuition you've been doubting is proven right. Trust the hunch. It's been ahead of you the whole time.

interpretations.situation: An imbalance has been building — and you've been tolerating it longer than you should have. Maybe you're carrying more responsibility than your title reflects. Maybe your salary hasn't kept pace with your contribution. Maybe someone less qualified got the promotion. The Justice card says: you're not imagining the gap. It's real, and it's reaching its tipping point.
interpretations.energy: Justice demands clarity and honest accounting — starting with yourself. What are you actually worth in the current market? Not what you hope, not what you fear — the real number. This card says the most powerful move you can make right now is radical honesty about your value and your situation. Stop negotiating with yourself before you even reach the table.
interpretations.outcome: What you deserve arrives in proportion to your effort and your courage to claim it. Justice in outcome position is the card of overdue corrections — the raise, the title, the respect that should have come earlier. The scales don't just tip in your favor; they settle there. The adjustment, once it comes, is lasting.

interpretations.situation: All the pieces are already on the table — your skills, your network, your experience, even the opportunity. The only thing missing is the decision to combine them. The Magician appears for people who are closer to their next move than they realize. You're not lacking resources. You're lacking the moment of 'I'm doing this.'
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of resourcefulness, initiative, and deliberate action. The Magician says: stop waiting for the perfect moment, the perfect CV, or the perfect role. Take what you have — your 70%-ready application, your 3 relevant contacts, your slightly-outdated portfolio — and put it into motion. The Magician's power isn't in having perfect tools; it's in the willingness to use imperfect ones with conviction.
interpretations.outcome: A skilled, well-timed move turns your existing resources into something that surprises everyone — including you. The Magician in outcome position is the card of the person who gets the offer despite not checking every box, who negotiates above range because they presented their story with clarity and confidence. Mastery isn't having more — it's using what you have better than anyone expected.

interpretations.situation: Two forces are pulling you in opposite directions — and neither alone is the answer. Maybe it's stability vs. growth, passion vs. pay, or staying in your city vs. relocating for a better role. You've been oscillating between them, and the tension has become exhausting. Temperance appears when the solution isn't choosing one side, but finding the blend.
interpretations.energy: This is the energy of patience, precision, and creative synthesis. Temperance says: the either/or framing is a trap. The question isn't 'Should I stay or go?' — it's 'What combination of elements would make me feel both secure and alive?' That combination exists. It just takes more imagination than a binary choice, and more patience than an impulsive leap.
interpretations.outcome: A quiet alchemy — two things that seemed incompatible merge into something new and unexpectedly powerful. Temperance in outcome position means you find the rare role that combines what you need with what you want: the salary and the meaning, the stability and the growth, the challenge and the balance. It's not compromise. It's a higher-order solution.
